questions.question: Explain the difference between ArrayList and LinkedList with respect to performance characteristics and typical use cases.
keyPoints: ArrayList provides O(1) random access but O(n) insertion/deletion in middle
LinkedList provides O(1) insertion/deletion at ends but O(n) random access
ArrayList is preferred for frequent access by index
LinkedList is preferred for frequent additions/removals at beginning/middle
ArrayList uses dynamic array, LinkedList uses doubly-linked list

questions.question: Compare and contrast HashMap, LinkedHashMap, and TreeMap implementations. Discuss their ordering characteristics, performance, and typical use cases.
keyPoints: HashMap provides no ordering guarantees with O(1) performance
LinkedHashMap maintains insertion/access order with O(1) performance
TreeMap maintains sorted key order with O(log n) performance
HashMap is most efficient for general key-value storage
LinkedHashMap is used when insertion/access order matters
TreeMap is used when sorted keys or range operations are needed
All allow null values but TreeMap doesn't allow null keys
HashMap and LinkedHashMap are not thread-safe
